Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 021 [ritual against bad dreams][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] LU₂ ina MAŠ₂.GE₆-šu₂ IGI.IGI⸢mar⸣

(o 1) [...] a man keeps seeing [...] in his dream

o 22

[...] IL₂-⸢šu₂⸣

(o 2) [...] he lifts it

o 33

[...] ⸢NU⸣ lu₂TUR ša₂ DUḪ.LAL₃ DU₃uš

(o 3) [...] you make a wax figurine of a child;

o 44

[... MAŠ].SILA₃ GUB₃-šu₂ SARar₂ ana IGI dUTU KEŠ₂ KEŠ₂

(o 4) you write [...] (on) its left hip; you set out the ritual assemblage in front of Šamaš;

o 55

[... šim]LI GARan KAŠ u GEŠTIN BALqi₂

(o 5) you set up [...] of burāšu-juniper; you pour a libation of beer and wine;

o 66

[ana] ⸢IGI⸣ dUTU ki-a-am DU₁₁.GA

(o 6) you speak thus in front of Šamaš:

o 77

⸢EN₂⸣ dUTU ana di₂-gi₃-gi₃ suḫ₄-ḫu-ru pa-nu!(NI-)ka

(o 7) Incantation: "Šamaš, your face is turned towards the Igigi, your face lies upon Aya, the bride.

o 88

ana da-a kal-lat pa-nu-ka šak-na

o 99

ra-aš₂-bu kun-nu-u₂ i-lit-ti da-nu-um

(o 9) Awe-inspiring (god), beloved (god), offspring of Anum!

o 1010

ana-ku NENNI₂ A NENNI₂ ša₂ lu₂TUR ina MAŠ.GE₆-ia₅ KI-ia₅ rak-su-ma la₃ DU₈

(o 10) I am so-and-so, descendant of so-and-so, to whom a child is bound in my dream, and will not be released. I have faced you Šamaš, great lord, for the sake of the separation".

o 1111

dUTU EN GALu₂ aš₂-šu₂ KU₅si am-ḫur-ka

o 1212

an-nam ana IGI dUTU 3šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma NU šu-a-tu₂

(o 12) You recite this three times in front of Šamaš and the distressed one raises that figurine in his left hand (then) in his right (hand). He addresses Šamaš; Šamaš listens (to him); he swears (by) his word and speaks thus to the substitute, to the figurine:

o 1313

lu₂TU.RA ina ŠU-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30-šu₂ IL₂-ma ina 15-šu₂

o 1414

ana dUTU DU₁₁.DU₁₁ dUTU i-šem-me INIM-su

o 1515

i-ta-ma-ma a-na GABA.RI ana NU ki-a-am DU₁₁.GA

o 1616

EN₂ at-ta man-nu ša₂ ina MAŠ₂.GE₆ u bi-ri IGI.IGIru KI-ia₅

(o 16) Incantation: "You, whoever you are who always appear by me in dream and vision, now, in front of Šamaš, you are taken away, you are driven away and you are chased off.

o 1717

ina-an-na ina IGI dUTU tab-la-ta ek-<me>-ta u kuš-šu-da-ta

o 1818

u₂-lid-ka EDIN GIM a-bi-ka

(o 18) The open country begot you, as your father (did).

o 1919

u₂-kan-ni-ka qer-bet GIM um-mi-ka

(o 19) The meadows took care of you, as your mother (did).

o 2020

mut-tal-lik qer-be₂-e-tu₂ muš-te-ʾu-u₂ ur-ḫe-e-tu₂

(o 20) Roamer of the meadows, explorer of the paths, you are indeed the one who blocks journey(s).

o 2121

mu-šap-ri-su al-lak-ta at-ta-ma

o 2222

is-suḫ-ka-ma ina SU-ia₅ dUTU DI.KU₅ DINGIR u LU₂

(o 22) Šamaš, judge of god and man, tore you out of my body.

o 2323

niš DINGIR-MEŠ na-as-ḫa-ta-ma a-lak-ta-ka KU₅us

(o 23) (By) the life of the gods, you are torn out and may he block your journey!

o 2424

[...] ⸢dEREŠ?⸣.KI.GAL ina qi₂-bi-ti-ša₂ a-lak-ta-ka KU₅us

(o 24) [...] Ereškigal, at her command, may he block your journey!

o 2525

[... d]ASAR.LU₂.ḪI MAŠ.MAŠ DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

(o 25) [...] Asalluhi, incantation priest of the great gods, [...] may he block your journey [...] of Ea". Incantation formula.

o 2626

[...] E ša₂ dIDIM a-lak-ta-ka KU₅us te EN₂

o 2727

[...] x 3šu₂ ana NU DU₁₁.GA-ma

(o 27) You recite [...] ... three times to the figurine, and you wash [...] on top of it, on top of the figurine.

o 2828

[...] ⸢ana⸣ UGU-šu₂ ana UGU NU LUḪsi

o 2929

[...]-ma ana UB BAD₃ te-qeb-ber-šu₂ ZI₃.SUR.RAa ša₂ ZI₃ ŠE.MUŠ₅

(o 29) [...] and you bury it in the corner of the city wall; [...] a magic circle of šigūši-flour; [...] is separated on that very day.1

o 3030

[...] ina u₄-me-šu-ma KU₅is

o 3131

[...] x GU LA ana UGU ḪURir1

(o 31) You draw [...] ... on top (of it);

o 3232

[...] KI-šu₂ IGI.IGI ina UGU tu-šar-kab

(o 32) [...] keeps appearing by him: you make (it) ride on top of (it);

o 3333

[...] dUTU ŠIDnu e-ma ŠIDnu-u₂

(o 33) you recite [...] (in front of) Šamaš; [...] whatever you recite ... until the day of (his) recovery.

o 3434

[...] x ŠU a-di u₄-um TI.LA

o 3535

[... IGI.IGI]mar NU ana ID₂ ŠUBdi

(o 35) He keeps appearing [...]; you throw the figurine into the river.

o 3636

[...] ta-nam-bi la₃ le-ʾa DU₈?

(o 36) You name [...]; you release the powerless;

o 3737

[...] RU 3šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma

(o 37) You recite [...] three times and [...] and you make (him) take (it) on top of it.

o 3838

[...] x-ma ina muḫ-ḫi-šu₂ tuš-ta-ḫaz

o 3939

[...] AD NE ina ŠA₃ DUak

(o 39) [...] ... you go in the middle;

o 4040

[...] ID₂ ŠUBdi

(o 40) you throw [...] (into) the river.

(rest of obverse missing)
oo 2 or 3 missing lines according to editor
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] ... [...] ...

(r 1') [...] ... [...] ...

r 2'2'

[...] x DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ UŠ₂-MEŠ

(r 2') [...] ... his sons die

r 3'3'

[... a]-⸢lak⸣-tu₂ la₃ ku-šir₄ DU-MEŠ

(r 3') [...] he keeps going on unsuccessful journeys;

r 4'4'

[...] x a-na BA.UŠ₂-ia NA BI E₂ TI.LA šu-u₂ <<TI>> BA.UŠ₂

(r 4') [...] ... for my death. (As for) that man, he will die (in) the house where he lives.

r 5'5'

[...] a-lak-ta-šu₂ ana la-ma-di-ka

(r 5') [...] in order (for you) to learn his way

r 6'6'

[...] ⸢ka⸣-a-a-nam sad-rat-su

(r 6') [...] is constantly regular for him

r 7'7'

[...] i-da-ba-bu-ma

(r 7') [...] they speak and [...] strife afflicts him

r 8'8'

[...] ⸢pu⸣-uḫ-pu-uḫ-ḫu-u₂ GAR-šu₂

r 9'9'

[...] x NA BI ḪUL DAB-su

(r 9') [...] ... (as for) that man, evil will seize him.

r 10'10'

[...]-ma ana LU₂ u E₂-šu₂ NU TEe

(r 10') [...] and will not approach the man and his house.

r 11'11'

[...] dUTU.ŠU₂.A KI.GAR tu-qad-daš 7 ŠE.BAR KU₃.BABBAR

(r 11') [...] at sunset, you purify a clay pit; 7 grains of silver, [...], 7 grains of copper, 7 grains of tin, 7 grains of lead, carnelian, lapis-lazuli [...] agate, papparmīna-stone, flour: you throw (them) into the clay pit. You say the name of the distressed one.

r 12'12'

[...] ⸢7⸣ ŠE.BAR URUDU 7 ŠE.BAR AN.NA 7 ŠE.BAR A.GAR₅ na₄GUG na₄ZA.GIN₃

r 13'13'

[...] ⸢na₄⸣BABBAR.DILI na₄BABBAR.MIN₅ ZI₃.DA ana KI.GAR ŠUBdi MU lu₂GIG MUar₂

r 14'14'

⸢a?-na?⸣ dUTU.E₃ IM KID₂iṣ NU NITA u MUNUS DU₃uš NU NITA₂ ut-ta-pa-aṣ!?

(r 14') At sunrise, you break off clay; you make the figurines of a man and a woman; the figurine of the man is pushed away.

r 15'15'

TA GAR-šu NU MUNUS ku?-zu?-li-tu₂ ta-bar-ši IM.GA₂.LI ŠEŠ₂-su-nu-ti NU NITA₂ IGI 15-šu₂

(r 15') After you have set it up, you select the figurine of the woman, the throne-bearer; you annoint them (with) orpiment. You make the male's figurine right eye iridescent; you make his left eye tiqqûtu-coloured;

r 16'16'

za-ri-qu-tu₂ tu-ša₂-lak IGI GUB₃-šu₂ ti-qu-tu₂ tu-ša₂-lak

r 17'17'

TUG₂ U₄-1-KAM MU₄.MU₄-su NU MUNUS TUG₂ GUN₃.A MU₄.MU₄-si IGI!(ŠI) dUTU ina gišAŠ.TE TUŠ-šu₂-nu-tu₂ šum₃-šu₂-nu

(r 17') you clothe it in a one day garment. (As for) the figurine of the woman, you clothe it with a multicoloured garment; you sit them down on a throne before Šamaš; you write their names on their left hips. The name of the man (is): "That evil man will seize the breast".

r 18'18'

ina MAŠ.SILA₃ GUB₃-šu₂-nu SARar₂ MU NITA LU₂ ḪUL BI UBUR DAB-ma

r 19'19'

MU MUNUS DAM LU₂ ḪUL BI UBUR DAB-ma 7 NINDA TUR.TUR

(r 19') The name of the woman (is): "That evil man's wife will seize the breast". You place 7 small loaves. As for the Lamaštu-demonesses who are fearful, you place at their disposal mirsu-cake and butter bread; you strew animal fat, sweet oil, filtered oil, pure oil over their heads. After you have done this for him, you set up an offering table, an imhur-ešra-plant (and) a censer of burāšu-juniper; you present the pure offering (and) beer.

r 20'20'

GAR dDIM₃.ME-MEŠ ša₂ LUḪ-MEŠ NINDA.I₃.DE₂.A NINDA I₃.NUN.NA

r 21'21'

ana IGI-šu₂-nu GARan I₃.UDU I₃ DU₁₀.GA I₃ ḫal-ṣa I₃ SIKIL.LA

r 22'22'

ana SAG.DU-šu₂-nu DUB TA an-nam te-te-ep-šu₂ GI.DU₈ IGI.20

r 23'23'

NIG₂.NA šimLI GAR uduSISKUR eb-ba <<KAŠ BALqi₂>> KAŠ BALqi₂2

r 24'24'

uzuZA₃ uzuME.ḪE₂ uzuKA.IZI tu-ṭaḫ-ḫe te-re-qam-ma

(r 24') You bring shoulder, fatty tissue and roast meat; you step back and you remove the figurine of the man from his right, the figurine of the woman from his left; you seize the distressed one's hand and you make him speak as follows:

r 25'25'

NU NITA₂ ina 15-šu₂ NU MUNUS ina GUB₃-šu₂ GIŠ-ma ŠU lu₂GIG DAB-ma kam tu-šad-bab-šu₂3

(colophon)
r 26'26'

IM <x> MU-MEŠ ul qa-ti

(r 26') Tablet, (n) lines. Not completed.

r 27'27'

IM mBAša₂-a bu₁₂-kur₂ mdINANA—MU—KAM ŠA₃.BAL.BAL mE₂.KUR—za-kir

(r 27') Tablet of Iqiša, son of Ištar-šum-ereš, descendant of Ekur-zakir.

1One sign is erased after LA, and "ana" has been written over the erasure.

2A sign has been erased after eb-ba.

3According to von Weiher, GIŠ=tabālu is attested once (SpTU 2, p. 108).

1 The end of the sentence probably means that the "child " is separated from the "distressed one".

Created by Marie-Françoise Besnier for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2011 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P348626
